domains.name: AI & Machine Learning
domains.description: Strategic domain covering model training, inference serving, vector storage, and AI gateway routing. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the AI Platform / MLOps team, who owns the HOW decisions for model endpoints,  prompt safety firewalls, and vector query tools.

capabilities.name: AI Gateway
capabilities.description: Outbound LLM/AI request traffic is routed, authenticated, rate-limited, and audited at a managed proxy layer that enforces safety, prompt templating, and budget/cost metrics.

capabilities.name: Model Inference & Serving
capabilities.description: Runtimes and infrastructure for deploying, hosting, and serving machine learning and large language model weights via standard query APIs.

domains.name: Analytics
domains.description: Strategic domain covering analytical processing and reporting over operational and business data. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the data team, who owns the HOW decisions for analytics and reporting technology.

capabilities.name: Analytics
capabilities.description: Operational and business data is processed and analyzed to produce insight through a managed analytics platform.

capabilities.name: Reporting
capabilities.description: Curated metrics and datasets are presented to consumers through managed reports and dashboards.

domains.name: Compute & Runtime
domains.description: Strategic domain covering application runtimes, serverless functions, and physical or virtual compute resources.

capabilities.name: Application Runtime
capabilities.description: First-party application code executes on a managed runtime that provides the process, web, or worker execution environment.

capabilities.name: Caching
capabilities.description: Frequently accessed data is stored in a fast, ephemeral tier to reduce latency and load on the system of record.

capabilities.name: Compute Platform
capabilities.description: Compute substrate or virtualized platform used to run Hosts.

implementations.notes: Example workspace maps EC2 to the compute platform capability.

capabilities.name: Container Orchestration
capabilities.description: Management of containerized workload lifecycles.

capabilities.name: Messaging
capabilities.description: Asynchronous messages and events are accepted, queued, and delivered between producers and consumers.

capabilities.name: Operating System
capabilities.description: Supported operating system product used to define managed Hosts.

capabilities.name: Patch Management
capabilities.description: Patch orchestration and update application capability for managed runtime components.

capabilities.name: Serverless Function Runtime
capabilities.description: Event-driven, scale-to-zero compute runtime capability.

capabilities.name: Service Mesh
capabilities.description: Service-to-service traffic is routed, secured, and observed through a dedicated connectivity and policy layer.

domains.name: Data
domains.description: Strategic domain covering data protection and resilience. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the data and storage team, who owns the HOW decisions for encryption implementation and backup and recovery platforms.

capabilities.name: Data Persistence
capabilities.description: Structured application data is durably stored, queried, and managed in a database or persistence platform.

capabilities.name: Data Resilience
capabilities.description: Resilience of data against loss or corruption through backup, restore, replication, and recovery capabilities.

capabilities.name: Encryption At Rest
capabilities.description: Protection of persisted data through encryption or equivalent storage safeguards.

capabilities.name: File Storage
capabilities.description: Files are durably stored and accessed through a shared file system or file storage interface.

capabilities.name: Object Storage
capabilities.description: Unstructured objects and blobs are durably stored and retrieved through an object storage interface.

domains.name: Identity & Access Management
domains.description: Strategic domain covering authentication and authorization. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the IAM team, who owns the HOW decisions for identity provider selection and access control model.

capabilities.name: Access Control Model
capabilities.description: Authorization model that controls access to a service or data platform.

capabilities.name: Authentication
capabilities.description: Identity and access authentication capability for users, services, administrators, or workloads.

domains.name: Integration
domains.description: Strategic domain covering the movement of messages, files, and data between systems. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the platform engineering team, who owns the HOW decisions for email delivery, managed file transfer, and data integration technology.

capabilities.name: Data Integration
capabilities.description: Data is moved, transformed, and synchronized between systems through a managed integration or ETL platform.

capabilities.name: Email Delivery
capabilities.description: Outbound and transactional email is accepted, routed, and delivered to recipients through a managed mail delivery service.

capabilities.name: File Transfer
capabilities.description: Files are exchanged between systems or partners reliably and securely through a managed file transfer service.

domains.name: Network
domains.description: Strategic domain covering network fabric infrastructure, connectivity, and segmentation. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the network team, who owns the HOW decisions for switching, routing, WAN, and traffic management technology.

capabilities.name: API Gateway
capabilities.description: Inbound API traffic is routed, authenticated, rate-limited, and transformed at a managed entry point in front of backend services.

capabilities.name: CDN
capabilities.description: Static and cacheable content is distributed and served from edge locations close to consumers to reduce latency and origin load.

capabilities.name: DNS
capabilities.description: Names are resolved to network addresses through authoritative and recursive domain name resolution.

capabilities.name: Network Connectivity
capabilities.description: Hosts and services can reach each other across the network fabric through approved switching and routing infrastructure.

capabilities.name: Network Segmentation
capabilities.description: Traffic between network zones is isolated and controlled by policy through VLANs, micro-segmentation, or software-defined networking.

capabilities.name: Traffic Management
capabilities.description: Application and network traffic is distributed, shaped, and controlled across services and infrastructure through approved load balancing and traffic management technology.

capabilities.name: WAF
capabilities.description: Inbound web traffic is inspected and filtered against application-layer threats through managed rule sets before reaching protected applications.

capabilities.name: WAN Connectivity
capabilities.description: Sites, data centers, and cloud environments are interconnected reliably through approved wide area network technology.

domains.name: Observability & Monitoring
domains.description: Strategic domain covering logging, metrics, tracing, and health monitoring across infrastructure and application stacks.

capabilities.name: Application Performance Monitoring
capabilities.description: Tracing and performance analysis of application runtimes.

capabilities.name: Health and Welfare Monitoring
capabilities.description: Runtime health, uptime, metrics, and operational welfare visibility.

capabilities.name: Log Management
capabilities.description: Aggregation, retention, searchability, and forwarding of system or application logs.

implementations.notes: Example OS configuration for centralized log forwarding.

domains.name: Security
domains.description: Strategic domain covering threat detection, security event monitoring, and secure credential management. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the security team, who owns the HOW decisions for threat response and secrets governance.

capabilities.name: Certificate Management
capabilities.description: Digital certificates are issued, distributed, renewed, and revoked through a managed certificate or PKI service.

capabilities.name: Secrets Management
capabilities.description: Secure storage, rotation, and access mediation for secrets and authenticators.

capabilities.name: Security Monitoring
capabilities.description: Threat detection, intrusion detection, security event monitoring, and audit telemetry.

domains.name: Software Delivery
domains.description: Strategic domain covering the build, integration, packaging, and configuration pipeline that delivers software into running environments. Capabilities in this domain are governed by the platform engineering team, who owns the HOW decisions for continuous integration and delivery, artifact custody, and configuration management technology.

capabilities.name: Artifact Management
capabilities.description: Build outputs, packages, images, and dependencies are stored, versioned, and served from a managed artifact repository.

capabilities.name: CI/CD Pipeline
capabilities.description: Source code is automatically built, tested, and promoted through environments by an automated continuous integration and delivery pipeline.

capabilities.name: Configuration Management
capabilities.description: System and application configuration is declared, applied, and reconciled across environments through a managed configuration platform.

domains.name: Testing & Quality
domains.description: Strategic domain covering all aspects of software testing, quality assurance, and release gates.

capabilities.name: Performance and Load Testing
capabilities.description: Capabilities to simulate load and measure system behavior under stress.

capabilities.name: Quality Gates
capabilities.description: Promotion criteria and automated checks required for lifecycle transitions.

capabilities.name: Test Authoring
capabilities.description: Tools and frameworks used to author automated tests.

capabilities.name: Test Execution and Automation
capabilities.description: Runtimes and orchestration services used to execute automated tests.
